data.title: 3 Income ETFs With Yields Above 20% for Investors to Watch
data.description:
Income‑focused ETFs have been gaining attention as more investors look for steady cash payouts in a volatile market. Some option‑income funds currently offer yields above 20%, driven by covered-call strategies and active options writing. According to TipRanks' ETF Screener, the YieldMax AI & Tech Portfolio Option Income ETF (GTPY), NestYield Dynamic Income Shield ETF (EGGY), and REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI) all offer dividend yields above 20%.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction AMZN bears now have this Tradr ETF

1. YieldMax AI & Tech Portfolio Option Income ETF (GTPY)

    GTPY focuses on big AI and tech names such as Nvidia (NVDA), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOGL), and AMD (AMD). Also, it uses an options strategy to turn market swings into weekly income. Since it writes calls on a basket of high‑volatility tech stocks, the fund can generate a very high yield. The trade‑off is that it may give up some upside if tech stocks run sharply higher.

    GTPY ETF offers a 37.78% dividend yield. It manages about $128.42 million in assets and charges a 1.06% expense ratio.

    The ETF makes weekly payouts. Its latest payment was $0.281 per share on July 23 and has paid $15.62 per share over the past year. The ETF has fallen about 11% over the past year.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.description:

Gold Royalty (GROY)

Gold Royalty is a precious metals royalty and streaming company. The company's royalty-based model helps avoid the heavy costs tied to running mines. Its growing portfolio of royalties allows it to benefit from rising gold prices without taking on the same day‑to‑day risks of running a mine. Also, strong gold demand and new royalty deals support an improved cash flow position and more projects moving into production.

data.title: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) Gains After Launching New Server Line Powered by AMD EPYC 9006 CPUs
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (SMCI) rose over 2% on Thursday after the company introduced its new H15 server portfolio built on AMD's (AMD) latest 6th‑generation EPYC 9006 Series processors. The launch expands Supermicro's data center lineup at a time when demand for high-performance compute systems continues to surge. Also, the rollout strengthens Supermicro's position in the fast‑growing AI infrastructure market.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The new H15 systems feature up to 256 cores and 512 threads, offering a major jump in compute power for customers running agentic AI, high‑performance computing, storage, and large enterprise applications.

The H15 line covers several system types, including dual‑socket Hyper servers for AI inference and cloud work, CloudDC systems built on OCP standards, high‑density GrandTwin and FlexTwin designs for hyperscale sites, and new Petascale Storage platforms for AI data lakes and analytics. Supermicro also updated its AMD GPU systems, adding new 5U PCIe servers that can support up to ten AMD Instinct MI350P GPUs.

Wide Range of H15 Systems and New AMD GPU Servers

Supermicro said the platform delivers a 1.7x performance boost over the prior generation. It also offers expanded memory bandwidth and improved compute density. The company said these gains help customers run more AI agents at the same time and speed up their business apps.

Vik Malyala, the company's Chief Business Officer, said the new AMD‑powered systems bring "high performance, rapid scalability, and peak efficiency" to customers deploying AI at scale. Also, AMD's Dan McNamara, general manager of Compute and Enterprise AI, noted that pairing EPYC CPUs with Instinct GPUs and Pensando networking helps enterprises deploy AI faster while lowering energy use and total cost.

Beyond individual servers, Supermicro is working with AMD on the Helios Platform, a rack‑scale system featuring 72 GPUs for large AI training jobs. The liquid‑cooled design combines EPYC CPUs, Instinct GPUs, Pensando networking, and AMD's ROCm software stack to support frontier‑level AI workloads.

Is SMCI a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, SMCI stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with four Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Super Micro Computer is $37.91, suggesting an upside of 21.50% from the current level.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Microsoft to Report Q4 Earnings on July 29; Here's Why Truist Is Bullish on MSFT Stock
data.description:
All eyes are now on tech giant Microsoft's (MSFT) fiscal fourth-quarter earnings on July 29 after Alphabet's (GOOGL) Q2 2026 print spooked investors due to concerns about its elevated capital spending. Heading into Q4 FY26 earnings, Truist analyst Terry Tillman reiterated a Buy rating on MSFT stock with a price target of $575. While MSFT stock has declined 21% year-to-date amid worries about returns on the company's massive AI spending, Tillman contends that the "Negative Sentiment Feels Overdone." He expects Microsoft to report "solid" Q4 performance and issue a strong outlook.

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Stumble as Trump Eyes Major Offensive Against Iran; Oil Spikes Above $100
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower on Thursday as escalating U.S.-Iran tensions drove oil prices above $100 per barrel.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • President Trump told Axios that he is considering a "massive attack" against Iran and will make a decision soon. He added that Israel "would join in two minutes" if asked, while also voicing concern about potential Iranian retaliation.
  • The Iran-backed Houthi rebel force attacked two Saudi tankers in the Bab al-Mandeb Strait, which handles roughly 12% of global seaborne oil flows. President Trump warned that further attacks would result in "major military punishment" for Iran and the Houthis.
  • Brent crude (BZ), the global oil benchmark, gained over 6% and climbed above $100 for the first time since late May.
  • Rising oil prices lifted the 10-year Treasury yield to its highest level since January 2025 and also raised rate hike odds. The Fed will meet for its next interest rate decision on July 29.
  • AI-related stocks sold off, with AMD (AMD), Intel (INTC), and Oracle (ORCL) closing in the red. However, Micron (MU) bucked the trend with a gain.
  • Alphabet (GOOGL) closed in the red after reporting its second-quarter earnings. Investor concerns centered around the company raising its 2026 capex guidance to $195-$200 billion, up from its prior estimate of $180-$190 billion.
  • Tesla (TSLA) faced pressure for similar reasons. The electric vehicle leader said capex climbed 142% year-over-year in the second quarter and guided for over $25 billion for the whole year.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: INTC Earnings: Intel Stock Soars after Crushing Q2 Earnings and Outlook
data.description:
Shares of Intel (INTC) soared in after-hours trading after the chipmaker reported earnings for its second quarter of Fiscal Year 2026. Earnings per share came in at $0.42, which crushed analysts' consensus estimate of $0.22 per share. Meanwhile, sales increased by 24.8% year-over-year, with revenue hitting $16.1 billion. This beat analysts' expectations of $14.43 billion.

Intel's Q3 2026 Outlook

Looking forward, management has provided the following guidance for Q3 2026:

  • Revenue between $15.8 billion and $16.8 billion, versus estimates of $15.16 billion
  • EPS of $0.38 compared to expectations of $0.28

As you can see, guidance was much better than expected, which also contributed to the stock's after-hours move.

Is Intel a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on 10 Buys, 22 Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average INTC price target of $114.93 per share implies 14.7% downside risk. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following today's earnings report. (See INTC Stock Forecast).
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Here's Why Hims & Hers Stock Soared 13% Today – July 23, 2026
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) surged as much as 13% on Thursday after the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) advisory panel voted to recommend loosening restrictions on a popular peptide compound, BPC‑157. This peptide has gained strong interest across wellness and telehealth markets. Investors see the move as a major opening for Hims, which has been preparing to grow its peptide offerings once regulations became clearer.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The FDA's Pharmacy Compounding Advisory Committee voted 8-6, with one abstention, to add the peptide BPC‑157 to the 503A Bulks List. This move lets state-licensed compounding pharmacies make custom versions of the peptide for patients. Also, the favorable vote came as a surprise. Earlier FDA briefing notes had raised safety concerns and suggested the committee might vote against the peptide.

The move aims to bring BPC‑157 out of the gray market and into regulated channels, allowing compounding pharmacies to make the peptide under close oversight.

For Hims, this shift could open a new revenue stream. The company already has a large customer base for wellness and performance products, and demand for peptides like BPC‑157 is rising fast. Also, the new offering could deepen customer engagement and lift average revenue per user.

Importantly, Hims has been making efforts to expand into peptide-based treatments. In 2025, the company acquired a specialized peptide manufacturing facility in California, giving it the ability to make these products in‑house. But it has held off on launching anything until the FDA set clearer rules. Now that the agency is moving to widen access, Hims is expected to speed up its plans and capture early demand.

Citi Lifts Target But Sees Limited Near‑Term Upside

Earlier today, Citi analyst Daniel Grosslight raised his price target on Hims & Hers to $35 from $28, but he also warned that expectations are already high going into the upcoming earnings report on August 10.

The analyst said he does not see much room for upside in the near‑term numbers, especially if the impact of the Eucalyptus acquisition is excluded. Even though the Novo (NVO) partnership should bring in strong membership growth, the shift toward monthly shipping and the company's still‑high churn rate will likely keep sales from breaking out.

Grosslight also said that Hims' newer areas, like TRT, HRT, and maybe peptides, should help growth over time. But he does not expect these gains to matter much this quarter.

Is Hims and Hers Stock a Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, HIMS stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 5 Buys and 11 Holds assigned in the last three months. At $29.22, the average Hims and Hers stock price target implies a 28.12% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: S&P 500 Top Stock Gainers Today, 7/23/26
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) finished lower on Thursday amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions and surging oil prices.

Let's break down the sectors behind the performance.

Today's Top-Performing Sector 

Industrials was the top-performing sector, driven higher by a slate of strong earnings reports. Allegion (ALLE) surged after reporting revenue growth of 13% to $1.15 billion, above the estimate of $1.12 billion. It also raised its full-year profit outlook to $8.85-$9.00, topping the consensus estimate of $8.79. Furthermore, Lockheed Martin (LMT) rallied after reporting second-quarter revenue of $20.06 billion, exceeding expectations of $19.34 billion. Competitor RTX (RTX) was also a top performer following an earnings beat.

Several industrial stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Allegion (ALLE)
  • Lockheed Martin (LMT)
  • United Rentals (URI)
  • RTX (RTX)
  • CSX (CSX)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the communication services sector faced the largest drawdown. Alphabet (GOOGL) closed in the red on concerns of overspending in AI after the tech giant reported its second-quarter numbers and guided for $195-$200 billion of capex this year, up from its prior estimate of $180-$190 billion. Furthermore, T-Mobile (TMUS) plummeted after its second-quarter revenue of $22.8 billion fell short of expectations for $22.9 billion.

Notable communication services stocks trading lower include:

  • T-Mobile (TMUS)
  • Alphabet (GOOGL)
  • Comcast (CMCSA)
  • Trade Desk (TTD)
  • News Corp (NWS)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'It's Insane,' Says Top Investor About Intel Stock
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) has taken investors on quite a ride this year. After soaring about 280%, the stock stumbled over the past month as investors locked in gains and concerns grew that chip valuations had outpaced fundamentals. The shares are now trading about 27% below their June peak, yet still up 177% for the year.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

There are still reasons for optimism. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company delivered another strong quarter while offering an upbeat outlook, reinforcing confidence that demand from cloud providers and enterprise customers remains solid.

Meanwhile, Intel recently announced Fortinet as the first publicly disclosed external customer for its foundry business under CEO Lip-Bu Tan, marking an important milestone as Intel works to establish itself as a contract chip manufacturer.

The next test will come today after the closing bell, when Intel reports second-quarter results. Wall Street expects another quarter of double-digit revenue growth, giving investors a clearer picture of whether the turnaround continues to gain momentum after one of the market's biggest rallies this year.

However, it's not enough for top investor Jonathan Weber, who believes that INTC's "insane" valuation is more sizzle than steak.

"Going into the Q2 earnings report, Intel is way too expensive for my taste," explains the 5-star investor, who is among the top 2% of stock pros covered by TipRanks. 

Weber notes that Intel's expected revenue and EPS for Q2 are nothing spectacular. He points out that annualizing the expected quarterly EPS of $0.22 amounts to just $0.88 per share. For a company trading at more than $100 a share, the investor doesn't find this all that impressive.

Moreover, while estimated top-line growth of some 12% can be considered "solid," it pales in comparison to the numbers that firms like Broadcom (247%), Nvidia (82%), or AMD (43%) are delivering.

"During a phase when many chip companies see explosive growth on the back of AI tailwinds, a 12% revenue growth rate is pretty meager on a comparative basis, I believe," adds Weber, who points out that Intel's growth rate is the lowest of the bunch.

Intel is leading the pack when it comes to one figure, however, with a forward price-to-earnings multiple of 95x that is head and shoulders above many of its peers. Though the investor isn't necessarily bearish on AI names, he's also aware that risks of a bubble are present.

Weber argues that if negativity starts to catch on, it could have some severe repercussions for Intel and its sub-10% margins.

"Its valuation is very high, so if there is a bubble burst, it has a lot of room to fall," he worries. And that leads Weber to conclude that it's better to look elsewhere.

Weber is therefore assigning a Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating for INTC shares. (To watch Weber's track record, click here)

That's right around where Wall Street finds itself as well. With 10 Buys, 22 Holds, and 2 Sells, INTC carries a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Its 12-month average price target of $114.93 points to an upside of ~12%. (See INTC stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: "The Pullback Opens the Door": Freedom Broker Analyst Upgrades Alphabet Stock (GOOGL) Despite AI Spending Concerns
data.description:
Freedom Broker analyst Saken Ismailov upgraded Alphabet (GOOGL) stock to Buy from Hold and reaffirmed a $400 price target. The call comes even as investors continue to worry about Alphabet's rising capex and a rare dip into negative free cash flow. GOOGL stock is down about 7% in Thursday's regular trading session.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ismailov said Alphabet delivered strong Q2 results, with both revenue and operating income beating Wall Street estimates. He highlighted faster growth in Google Cloud, steady ad demand, and improving enterprise AI monetization as key drivers of the quarter. According to the analyst, the results show the ongoing strength of Search and the rising value of Alphabet's AI tools for business customers.

Still, the report noted several pressure points. Alphabet's capital spending jumped again, pushing free cash flow into negative territory for the quarter. The company also raised its 2026 capex outlook to a range of $195 billion to $205 billion, a sign that infrastructure and AI‑related investments will stay high. Ismailov said this level of spending will likely weigh on operating margins and cash generation over the next two years.

Because of this, he increased revenue forecasts for 2026 and 2027 but cut the operating margin estimates to reflect higher infrastructure costs and depreciation. The analyst called the quarter "neutral overall," saying the strong top‑line performance was balanced out by the heavier investment load.

Even so, Ismailov said Alphabet's recent drop makes the stock more appealing for long-term buyers. He said Alphabet's main businesses are still strong. Its AI plan is starting to show real gains, and Google Cloud keeps growing in a way that should help future profits.

Are Google Shares a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on Alphabet stock based on 29 Buys and five Holds assigned in the past three months. Further, the average GOOGL stock price target of $437.79 per share implies 37.66% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
